talk_title: Quantitative fluorescence protein assays and selective biomarkers for ADC choice in breast cancer

speaker.name: David Rim
speaker.affiliation: Yale School of Medicine, Department of Pathology and Medicine, Director of Yale Cancer Center Tissue Microarray Facility

targets.dependency_context: Advanced breast cancer treated with ADCs

biomarkers.name: HER2 quantitative fluorescence (attomoles/mm²)
biomarkers.finding: Higher HER2 levels correlated with longer T‑DXd benefit (median OS 12.8 mo vs 7.5 mo, HR per 50‑unit increase 0.70, p<0.05)

biomarkers.name: Trop‑2 quantitative fluorescence (attomoles/mm²)
biomarkers.finding: High Trop‑2 expression associated with poorer outcomes on T‑DXd (high vs low median OS 7.2 mo vs 13.4 mo, p<0.01)

models.name: Yale breast cancer surgical pathology cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: 316 prospectively collected breast cancer cases, HER2 IHC scores available

models.name: Retrospective Dana‑Farber T‑DXd cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: 51 metastatic breast cancer cases treated with trastuzumab‑deruxtecan

clinical_data.metric: Hazard ratio per 50‑unit increase in HER2 attomoles
clinical_data.population: metastatic breast cancer treated with T‑DXd

clinical_data.value: Top quartile median OS 6.2 mo vs bottom quartile 13.9 mo
clinical_data.population: metastatic breast cancer treated with T‑DXd

MOA_summary: Rim's laboratory developed the Troplex quantitative immunofluorescence platform that converts fluorescence intensity into absolute protein concentration (attomoles/mm²) for HER2, Trop‑2 and other membrane antigens. By standardizing staining with cell‑line calibrators, the assay achieves <10% CV and enables continuous measurement of target density, which correlates with ADC efficacy and informs a proposed selective biomarker framework for choosing between competing HER2‑ or Trop‑2‑directed ADCs.
key_findings: The Troplex assay quantifies HER2 and Trop‑2 in absolute units with inter‑ and intra‑assay CV <10% using cell‑line standards.
In a retrospective Yale cohort (n=316), quantitative HER2 levels distinguished patients likely to benefit from trastuzumab‑deruxtecan, with a hazard ratio of 0.70 per 50‑unit increase (p<0.05).
High Trop‑2 expression was associated with significantly shorter overall survival on T‑DXd (median OS 6.2 mo vs 13.9 mo for low Trop‑2, p<0.01).
Combined HER2‑high/Trop‑2‑low tumors showed the best outcomes on HER2‑directed ADCs, suggesting a selective biomarker strategy for drug choice.
The assay received New York State Department of Health (NYSDOH) CLIA approval, enabling clinical deployment.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: High Trop‑2 expression appears to blunt response to HER2‑directed ADCs, indicating a potential resistance mechanism or competitive target effect.
Patients with both high HER2 and high Trop‑2 had poorer outcomes than HER2‑high/Trop‑2‑low, supporting the need for a selective biomarker rather than a single target assessment.
open_questions: What is the optimal quantitative cutoff for HER2 or Trop‑2 to guide ADC selection?
How do intrinsic molecular subtypes (e.g., PAM50) influence the quantitative protein thresholds?
What is the contribution of the tumor microenvironment and adaptive immunity to ADC durability beyond target density?

cited_papers: JCO Precision Oncology 2024 Rim et al.
